title: Outport Configuration
description: Configuration file for Outport port manager (outport.yml)

name.description: Project name. Used to generate port hashes and hostnames. Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

open.description: Services to open by default with 'outport open'. When omitted, all services with a hostname are opened.

computed.description: Computed values derived from service ports and hostnames. Written to .env files alongside service ports.

env_var.description: Environment variable name for this service's port (e.g., PORT, DB_PORT).

preferred_port.description: Preferred port number. Used if available, otherwise falls back to hash-based allocation.

hostname.description: Hostname for .test domain (e.g., myapp.test, api.myapp.test). Must contain the project name. The .test suffix is optional in config but conventional.

env_file.description: Which .env file(s) to write this port to. Defaults to .env.

aliases.description: Named additional hostnames for this service. Each key is a label and the value is the hostname. Requires a primary hostname to be set.

value.description: Template string using ${service.field} syntax. Fields: port, hostname, url, env_var. Modifiers: url:direct. Standalone vars: ${project_name}, ${instance}. Conditionals: ${var:-default}, ${var:+replacement}.

env_file.description: Where to write the computed value. Can be a string, array of strings, or array of objects with file and value for per-file overrides.

value.description: Override template for this specific file.
